description: Monitor St Annes Old Links competition tee sheet and alert user when an early tee time becomes available.

env_vars.name: PLAYER_NAME
env_vars.description: Name as displayed on tee sheet

env_vars.name: CUTOFF_TIME
env_vars.description: Alert if player tee time is at or after this (HH:MM)

cli_flags.description: Test authentication only, print result, exit
Discover next Saturday competition compid, print, exit
Parse tee sheet, print all tee times and players, exit
Run full analysis, print result without sending email, exit
Send a test email to ALERT_TO, print result, exit
Save raw competition page HTML to debug_output.html

chunks.name: Project Scaffolding + Login
chunks.goal: Create project structure and implement member login with session cookie handling.
implementation_notes: Use requests.Session() to persist cookies
GET login page first to capture any hidden form fields or CSRF tokens
POST to https://www.stannesoldlinks.com/member_login with fields: login=MEMBER_ID, pin=MEMBER_PIN
Check response URL or content to confirm login success
Implement --test-login flag
pass_criteria: pip install -r requirements.txt completes without error
python3 scraper.py --test-login prints 'Login successful' and exits with code 0
.env.example contains all 9 required variable names
.gitignore includes .env, __pycache__, *.pyc, last_alert.json, debug_output.html
fail_criteria: pip install fails for any dependency
Login returns HTTP error or stays on login page

chunks.name: Competition Discovery
chunks.goal: Find the compid for the next upcoming Saturday competition via AJAX.
implementation_notes: POST to https://www.stannesoldlinks.com/competition2.php
Body: requestType=ajax, ajaxaction=compsfilter, fromdate=<today>, todate=<today+14days>
Parse response HTML for competition links matching pattern: /competition2.php?tab=details&compid=
Filter for competitions whose date falls on a Saturday (weekday() == 5)
Pick the nearest upcoming Saturday
Implement --find-comp flag
pass_criteria: python3 scraper.py --find-comp prints competition name, date, and compid
Printed date is a Saturday
compid is a numeric string
If no Saturday comp found, prints 'No upcoming Saturday competition found' and exits 0
fail_criteria: Printed date is not a Saturday
Wrong compid extracted (non-numeric or from a different date)

chunks.name: Tee Sheet Parsing
chunks.goal: Fetch the competition detail page and parse all tee times and player names.
implementation_notes: GET https://www.stannesoldlinks.com/competition2.php?tab=details&compid=<COMPID>
With --debug flag, save raw HTML to debug_output.html
Use BeautifulSoup to find tee time rows
Each row should have: time (HH:MM format) and 1-4 player name slots
Empty slots are blank/whitespace or a placeholder like '-'
Return list of dicts: [{time: '08:00', players: ['A Smith', '', 'B Jones', '']}]
Implement --parse-teesheet flag
pass_criteria: python3 scraper.py --parse-teesheet prints structured tee time rows with times and player names
debug_output.html is created when --debug flag is used
At least one tee time row is successfully parsed
Times are in HH:MM format
Empty player slots are represented as empty strings
fail_criteria: Script raises an unhandled exception or HTML parser error

chunks.name: Analysis + Deduplication Logic
chunks.goal: Compare player's tee time against cutoff and find available early slots. Deduplicate alerts.
implementation_notes: Search parsed tee sheet for row where any player name contains PLAYER_NAME (case-insensitive partial match)
Extract player's time as datetime.time object
Compare against CUTOFF_TIME (parse from HH:MM string)
If player_time >= cutoff_time: find rows where time < cutoff_time AND at least one player slot is empty
Build list of available early slots: [{time: '08:30', empty_slots: 2}]
Load last_alert.json; compare available slots list with previous run
Only trigger alert if slots have changed
Save current slots to last_alert.json
Implement --analyse flag (prints result, does not send email)
pass_criteria: python3 scraper.py --analyse correctly identifies player's tee time from tee sheet
Correctly lists tee time rows before CUTOFF_TIME with empty slots
Running --analyse twice with no tee sheet changes produces same output (no duplicate state)
last_alert.json is written after each --analyse run
Setting CUTOFF_TIME=23:59 causes all early slots to be identified
fail_criteria: Player's tee time not found (when player is on tee sheet)
Wrong time comparison (e.g. string comparison instead of time comparison)
Duplicate alert triggered on unchanged data

chunks.name: Email Alert + End-to-End
chunks.goal: Implement SMTP email sending and wire together the full run mode.
implementation_notes: Use smtplib.SMTP with STARTTLS for email sending
Email subject: 'Early tee time available - St Annes Old Links'
Email body: list available early slots with times and number of empty places
Implement --test-email flag: sends a test email with dummy content
Full run (no flags): login → find comp → parse tee sheet → analyse → send alert if needed
All log output via logging module with timestamps
Graceful error handling: catch and log exceptions, exit with code 1 on fatal error
pass_criteria: python3 scraper.py --test-email sends email and prints 'Email sent' and exits 0
Full run python3 scraper.py completes without unhandled exception
Email received contains correct tee time information when condition is met
python3 scraper.py >> /tmp/teetime.log 2>&1 produces clean timestamped log lines
Exit code 0 on success, 1 on fatal error
fail_criteria: Email not received within 2 minutes of send
Log output contains raw tracebacks (should be caught and logged cleanly)

overall_pass_criteria: Setting CUTOFF_TIME=23:59 causes an alert email to be sent
Email contains accurate tee time data from the live site
Running twice with unchanged tee sheet sends only one email
Full run completes in under 30 seconds
Cron log output is clean and timestamped
